---
title: "Yulia Tyukhova"
subtitle: "Independent researcher in lighting, human perception, and visual comfort."
page-layout: article
toc: false
comments: false
format:
  html:
    css: styles.css
---

::: {.column-page style="text-align: center; margin-bottom: 2em;"}
![](images/profile.jpeg){width=25%}

<div style="font-size: 1.5em; margin-top: 1em;">
<a href="https://www.linkedin.com/in/yuliatyukhova/" target="_blank"><i class="bi bi-linkedin"></i></a>
<a href="https://x.com/ytyukhova/" target="_blank"><i class="bi bi-twitter" style="margin-left: 0.5em;"></i></a>
<a href="https://github.com/ytyukhova/" target="_blank"><i class="bi bi-github" style="margin-left: 0.5em;"></i></a>
</div>
:::

I'm Yulia Tyukhova, an independent researcher focused on human perception, lighting quality, glare, visual comfort, and data analytics. My work spans research, standards, and applied studies in lighting and visibility.

I serve on technical and non-technical committees for the Illuminating Engineering Society (IES), the International Commission on Illumination (CIE), and the Transportation Research Board (TRB). I have chaired the IES Marks Award committee and previously led the IES Emerging Professional Program.

I author and review scientific papers, hold two patents, and present regularly at professional conferences.

Areas of focus include:

- **Human perception and visual comfort**
- **Glare evaluation and mitigation**
- **Lighting quality and luminance measurement**
- **Data-driven analysis for lighting research**
